fact: August 4 is exactly 216 days into the year - that's 6 cubed, making it a perfect cube number day!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is peak cricket chirping season - you can actually tell the temperature by counting their chirps and doing math!
August 4 falls smack in the middle of Leo season, when the constellation Leo the Lion is easiest to spot in the night sky.
This date marks when many trees start secretly preparing for fall - their roots begin storing extra sugar even though their leaves are still green!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, August 4 is deep winter, with some areas getting their coldest temperatures of the year.
Meteor showers love this time of year - the Perseid meteor shower is just getting started and will peak in about a week!
Many flowers like sunflowers and black-eyed Susans are at their most gigantic size right around August 4.
The moon phase on August 4 changes every year, but when it's a new moon on this date, you get the darkest, most star-filled skies of summer.
In northern places like Alaska, the sun is still setting super late - sometimes not until 10 PM or later!
August 4 is prime time for fireflies to put on their most spectacular light shows in backyards across North America.
